title: Saving NSF’s Workforce Act
plain_summary: This bill stops the National Science Foundation from firing workers or laying them off until Congress makes a complete budget for the agency in 2026. The only time they can still fire someone is if that person does something wrong at work or does a very bad job. The bill is meant to protect the jobs of people who work for this government science agency. It keeps workers safe from losing their jobs while Congress figures out the budget.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Saving NSF's Workforce Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Reduction in Force Moratorium at National Science Foundation
sections.text: The National Science Foundation cannot fire workers or lay off employees until Congress passes a full budget for the Foundation for the year two thousand twenty six. During this time, the Foundation is not allowed to start or carry out any reduction in force (laying off workers to cut costs). The Foundation also cannot fire any employee who works in the competitive service (jobs filled through testing and merit), the excepted service (certain specialized government jobs), or career employees in the Senior Executive Service (top level government managers). The only exception is when an employee is fired for cause, which means for misconduct, not doing their job properly, or being inefficient. The terms competitive service, excepted service, and career appointee have the same meanings as defined in sections twenty one oh two, twenty one oh three, and thirty one thirty two of title five of the United States Code. This rule works together with any other laws about taking negative actions against government employees, including chapter seventy five of title five.
